hero.headline: Alliance University Online MBA in Human Resource Management: AI-Integrated Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: Alliance University Online MBA with an HRM specialisation. NAAC A, UGC-DEB approved, NIRF #71, AICTE approved, WES recognised. HR Analytics using AI, Strategic Workforce Management, Technology Enabled Learning, Rewards and Performance Analytics, and Diversity Equity and Inclusion Metrics in Sem 3-4. Rs 1,75,000 effective total cost with zero-cost EMI through the university.

sections.tldr: Alliance Online MBA HRM: NAAC A, NIRF #71, Rs 1,75,000 effective total cost (tuition + registration + exam fees), zero-cost EMI direct from university. AI-integrated HR curriculum with HR Analytics using AI, Strategic Workforce Management, Rewards and Performance Analytics, and Diversity Equity and Inclusion Metrics. Cross-functional financial literacy via shared Sem 4 finance modules. Design Thinking mandatory in Sem 3.

about.body:
The Alliance University Online MBA in Human Resource Management is offered by Alliance University, Bangalore. Alliance holds NAAC A accreditation, NIRF #71 University ranking, AICTE approval, and WES recognition. The online programme is UGC-DEB approved.

The HRM specialisation delivers AI-integrated HR subjects in Sem 3: HR Analytics using AI, Strategic Workforce Management, Technology Enabled Learning, Rewards and Performance Analytics, and Diversity Equity and Inclusion Metrics. Design Thinking is mandatory for all Alliance MBA students in Sem 3.

A distinctive Alliance HRM feature: Sem 4 shares common modules with the Finance specialisation, adding cross-functional financial literacy to HR strategy. Alliance MBA HRM shares common Sem 4 modules with Finance, adding cross-functional financial literacy to HR strategy. HRM graduates gain exposure to Revenue Intelligence, Financial Analysis and Modelling, and M&A subjects alongside their HR depth. This cross-functional design prepares HR leaders for business partner roles where financial fluency is expected.

sem3Subjects.name: Design Thinking
sem3Subjects.description: Apply human-centred design frameworks to business problem identification, prototyping, and iterative solution development. Mandatory for all Alliance MBA specialisations.

sem3Subjects.name: HR Analytics using AI
sem3Subjects.description: Apply AI and machine learning models to workforce data for attrition prediction, hiring pipeline optimisation, and employee engagement scoring.

sem3Subjects.name: Strategic Workforce Management
sem3Subjects.description: Design workforce planning frameworks, succession pipelines, and talent supply-demand models aligned with organisational growth strategy.

sem3Subjects.name: Technology Enabled Learning
sem3Subjects.description: Evaluate LMS platforms, microlearning design, gamification frameworks, and AI-driven personalised learning paths for corporate L&D.

sem3Subjects.name: Rewards and Performance Analytics
sem3Subjects.description: Build compensation benchmarking models, variable pay structures, and performance measurement frameworks using data-driven analytics.

sem3Subjects.name: Diversity Equity and Inclusion Metrics
sem3Subjects.description: Define and measure DEI KPIs, build inclusion indices, and design evidence-based DEI interventions for organisations.

sem4Subjects.name: Strategic Management in Digital Era
sem4Subjects.description: Develop corporate strategy using digital transformation frameworks, competitive analysis in platform economies, and technology-driven growth planning.

sem4Subjects.name: Dissertation and Viva
sem4Subjects.description: Complete an applied HR research project with formal viva defence. Topics may cover AI-driven talent analytics, DEI programme effectiveness, or workforce planning models.

sem4Subjects.name: Revenue Intelligence for Fixed Income
sem4Subjects.description: Apply yield curve analysis and bond pricing frameworks. Provides cross-functional financial literacy for HR business partner roles.

sem4Subjects.name: Mergers Acquisition and Corporate Restructuring
sem4Subjects.description: Evaluate M&A workforce integration, due diligence on people costs, and post-merger cultural integration. Critical for HR leaders involved in M&A transitions.

sem4Subjects.name: Financial Analysis and Modelling
sem4Subjects.description: Build financial models and interpret financial statements. Essential skill for HR business partners presenting workforce ROI to CFOs and boards.

sem4Subjects.name: AI and Human Biases in Finance
sem4Subjects.description: Examine cognitive biases and AI decision-making limitations. Applicable to HR for understanding bias in AI-driven hiring and performance systems.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all Alliance MBA specialisations. Alliance MBA HRM shares common Sem 4 modules with Finance, adding cross-functional financial literacy to HR strategy. Design Thinking is a mandatory Sem 3 subject for all specs.

whoHires.body:
HRM MBA graduates from Alliance Online target these industries and roles:

**Industries:** IT and tech companies, BFSI, Manufacturing, Consulting firms, E-commerce, Pharmaceutical companies

**Entry-level roles:** HR Executive, Talent Acquisition Specialist, HR Analyst, L&D Coordinator, Compensation and Benefits Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** HR Business Partner, Talent Acquisition Manager, L&D Manager, HR Analytics Manager, DEI Programme Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** CHRO, VP Human Resources, Head of Talent, Director L&D, Chief People Officer

**Top hiring sectors:** Infosys, Wipro, TCS, HCL, Accenture, Deloitte, KPMG, Amazon, Flipkart, Bosch, Continental, Biocon, Razorpay

skills.body:
The Alliance HRM specialisation builds AI-augmented people management skills:

**Technical skills:**
- HR Analytics using AI: attrition prediction models, hiring funnel optimisation, employee engagement scoring using machine learning
- Rewards and Performance Analytics: compensation benchmarking, variable pay modelling, and performance measurement frameworks
- Diversity Equity and Inclusion Metrics: DEI KPI definition, inclusion index construction, and evidence-based intervention design
- Technology Enabled Learning: LMS evaluation, microlearning design, and AI-driven personalised learning path creation

**Cross-functional skills:**
- Financial Analysis and Modelling: interpreting financial statements and building workforce cost models for board presentations
- Mergers Acquisition and Corporate Restructuring: people-side M&A due diligence, workforce integration planning, and cultural integration

**Soft skills:** Design Thinking for employee experience redesign, strategic workforce planning for leadership conversations, DEI programme facilitation, and presenting HR ROI to CFOs and boards.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Alliance Online MBA HRM against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA HRM (Rs 2,25,000):** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 with strong all-India brand. Amity HRM covers organisational behaviour, talent management, and industrial relations. Alliance HRM at Rs 1,75,000 is Rs 50,000 cheaper and includes HR Analytics using AI and DEI Metrics as dedicated subjects. Alliance's AI-integrated HR curriculum is a genuine differentiator. For candidates wanting AI-augmented HR skills at lower fees, Alliance is a strong pick.

**vs Chandigarh University Online MBA HRM (Rs 1,65,000):** CU is NAAC A+ and NIRF #28 at Rs 10,000 lower fees. CU HRM covers standard HR domains. Alliance HRM adds AI-driven HR Analytics, Technology Enabled Learning, and cross-functional finance modules in Sem 4. CU is stronger for North India careers. Alliance is stronger for Bangalore-based tech companies and South India roles.

**vs Sharda University Online MBA HRM (Rs 1,54,500):** Sharda offers the lowest fees in this comparison at Rs 1,54,500. Sharda HRM covers core HR subjects. Alliance HRM at Rs 20,500 more adds AI-integrated HR Analytics, DEI Metrics, and cross-functional finance literacy. For candidates prioritising AI skills and Bangalore brand access, Alliance justifies the premium.

fees.body:
The Alliance University Online MBA in Human Resource Management is priced at Rs 1,60,000 total tuition fee across 4 semesters (Rs 40,000 per semester).

**Additional charges**:
- Registration fee: Rs 5,000 (one-time, non-refundable at admission)
- Examination fee: Rs 5,000 per year (Rs 10,000 total across 2 years)

**Effective total cost**: Rs 1,75,000 inclusive of tuition, registration, and exam charges.

**Payment options**:
- **Semester-wise**: Rs 40,000 per semester x 4 semesters
- **Full upfront**: Rs 1,60,000 one-time (plus Rs 5K reg + exam)

**Zero-cost EMI**: Available directly through Alliance University (no external marketing partner or NBFC intermediary). Contact our counsellor for current tenure options.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Alliance University each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by Alliance University, Bangalore, and carries the Alliance name with the MBA designation. The Human Resource Management specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. The degree is WES recognised for international credential evaluation. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Alliance Online MBA HRM students. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Meera
items.liked: HR Analytics using AI was the standout subject. I used the attrition prediction models directly in my current HR analytics role. The AI integration is built into the curriculum, not a surface-level addition.
items.disliked: Smaller alumni network compared to Amity or JAIN online programmes.
items.body: I work in HR analytics at a Bangalore tech firm and the Alliance HRM MBA gave me structured frameworks for AI-driven people analytics. The cross-functional finance modules in Sem 4 were unexpected but genuinely useful for HR business partner conversations with finance teams.

items.name: Rahul
items.liked: DEI Metrics and Strategic Workforce Management subjects were well-designed. Technology Enabled Learning helped me redesign our company's L&D programme.
items.disliked: Alliance's online brand is newer. Pune employers needed explanation about the university's credentials and NAAC A status.
items.body: Strong HRM curriculum with practical AI integration. The Rs 1.75L total cost is competitive. For Pune-based HR roles, I had to explain the Alliance credential more than I would with Amity. Content quality was high.

items.name: Ananya
items.liked: Rewards and Performance Analytics gave practical compensation benchmarking skills. Fees were competitive.
items.disliked: NAAC A is a step below A+ or A++. The specialisation list is smaller than universities offering 15+ options. Limited elective choices within HRM.
items.body: Decent programme for the price. The AI subjects are a differentiator that other HRM programmes at this price point do not offer. But for brand-conscious employers, the NAAC A grade and newer online presence are limitations to be aware of.

faqs.question: Is Alliance Online MBA really AI-integrated across all courses?
faqs.answer: Yes. Alliance embeds AI-specific subjects into every MBA specialisation, not as electives but as core semester subjects. The HRM spec includes HR Analytics using AI in Sem 3 and AI and Human Biases in Finance in Sem 4. This is built into the curriculum, not a separate certificate or add-on module.

faqs.question: What is the total effective cost of Alliance Online MBA HRM?
faqs.answer: Rs 1,75,000. This includes Rs 1,60,000 tuition (Rs 40,000 per semester x 4), Rs 5,000 one-time registration fee, and Rs 10,000 total examination fees (Rs 5,000 per year x 2 years).

faqs.question: Does Alliance offer zero-cost EMI for the Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Yes. Zero-cost EMI is available directly through Alliance University with no external marketing partner or NBFC intermediary. Contact our counsellor for current tenure options and approval terms.

faqs.question: Why does Alliance HRM share Sem 4 modules with Finance?
faqs.answer: Alliance MBA HRM shares common Sem 4 modules with Finance to build cross-functional financial literacy. HR leaders increasingly serve as business partners who present workforce ROI to CFOs and boards. Subjects like Financial Analysis and Modelling and M&A help HRM graduates handle budgeting, headcount cost modelling, and workforce integration during mergers.

faqs.question: Does Alliance HRM cover HR Analytics and DEI?
faqs.answer: Yes. HR Analytics using AI is a dedicated Sem 3 subject covering attrition prediction, hiring pipeline optimisation, and engagement scoring using AI models. Diversity Equity and Inclusion Metrics is a separate Sem 3 subject covering DEI KPI definition, inclusion indices, and evidence-based DEI interventions. Both are core subjects, not electives.

chooseIf: You want an AI-integrated HRM MBA with dedicated subjects on HR Analytics using AI, DEI Metrics, and Technology Enabled Learning at Rs 1,75,000 total cost.
You are targeting HR business partner, talent analytics, or L&D leadership careers in South India or Bangalore tech companies.
You want cross-functional financial literacy built into the HRM curriculum for board-level HR business partner roles.
consider: If NAAC grade is a primary filter, Amity (A+) or JAIN (A++) carry higher accreditation grades at higher fees.
If North India HR careers are the goal, CU HRM at Rs 1,65,000 with NAAC A+ and NIRF #28 is a stronger regional pick.
If the lowest fee is the priority, Sharda HRM at Rs 1,54,500 is Rs 20,500 cheaper but lacks AI-integrated HR subjects.
